data.title: Baby Bunting Group H1 2026 EPS Rebound Tests Bullish Margin Expansion Narrative
data.description:
Baby Bunting Group (ASX:BBN) opened H1 2026 with H2 2025 revenue of A$267.6 million and basic EPS of A$0.042, capped by trailing 12 month revenue of A$539.0 million and EPS of A$0.056. The company has seen revenue move from A$249.9 million in H2 2024 to A$254.4 million in H1 2025 and A$267.6 million in H2 2025, while basic EPS shifted from a loss of A$0.007 in H2 2024 to A$0.029 in H1 2025 and A$0.042 in H2 2025. This sets up a result where modestly higher margins are front and center for investors.

See our full analysis for Baby Bunting Group.

TTM margin at 1.4% keeps profits thin

  • Over the last 12 months, Baby Bunting earned A$7.5 million of net income on A$539.0 million of revenue, which works out to a 1.4% net margin compared with 1.2% the prior year.
  • Consensus narrative points to Store of the Future refurbishments and private label growth as margin drivers, and the step up from a loss of A$1.0 million in H2 2024 to A$5.6 million profit in H2 2025 supports that view. However, the 1.4% margin indicates those initiatives are feeding through only gradually rather than transforming profitability overnight.
    • Revenue moved from A$249.9 million in H2 2024 to A$267.6 million in H2 2025 while net income over that period swung from a loss to A$5.6 million profit, so more of each sales dollar is now falling to the bottom line but off a low base.
    • Analysts expect margins to reach around 4.0% in a few years, so the current 1.4% level leaves a wide gap that depends on refurbishments, digital channels and higher margin product mix continuing to perform as expected.

    • Within the current year, net income improved from A$3.9 million in H1 2025 to A$5.6 million in H2 2025, while trailing 12 month net income of A$7.5 million is below the A$9.5 million figure referenced in the bullish narrative, so the recent data is slightly softer than the most optimistic scenario assumes.
    • Forecast earnings growth of 37.8% per year and bullish expectations of A$29.7 million of earnings by 2028 are a big step up from today’s A$7.5 million, so anyone leaning on the bullish view needs to be comfortable that the recent EPS recovery can keep compounding from this base.

data.title: Soken Chemical & Engineering Q3 Net Margin Compression Challenges Bullish Earnings Narratives
data.description:
Soken Chemical & Engineering (TSE:4972) has put out its Q3 2026 scorecard, reporting revenue of ¥12.1b and basic EPS of ¥74.11, alongside net income of ¥1,230m. The company’s quarterly revenue has moved from ¥10.9b in Q3 2025 to ¥12.4b in Q4 2025 and then to ¥12.1b this quarter. Over the same period, basic EPS has shifted from ¥69.48 to ¥25.45 and then to ¥74.11, leaving investors to weigh earnings momentum against pressure on margins and to consider how durable that trend appears.

See our full analysis for Soken Chemical & Engineering.

With the headline numbers on the table, the next step is to compare them with the prevailing narratives around Soken Chemical & Engineering to see which views line up with the data and which ones are challenged.

Curious how numbers become stories that shape markets? Explore Community Narratives

Advertisement

Margins Ease Back To 7.9% Net Level

  • Over the last 12 months, Soken Chemical & Engineering earned ¥3,743m on ¥47,300m of revenue, which works out to a 7.9% net margin compared with 9.3% a year earlier.
  • What stands out for a bullish view is that this 7.9% margin sits alongside a five year earnings growth rate of 13.7% a year and forecast earnings growth of 8.33% a year, so:
    • Supporters can point to that 13.7% earnings CAGR and the Q3 2026 net income of ¥1,230m as evidence that profit generation has been solid over multiple periods, even with some margin compression.
    • At the same time, the pullback from a 9.3% margin to 7.9% gives you a clear data point to check whether this earnings growth rate and the Q3 EPS of ¥74.11 per share are being achieved with tighter profitability than before.

Revenue Growth Trails Broader Japan Market

  • On a trailing basis, revenue has been growing at about 2.6% a year to ¥47,300m, which is below the Japan market forecast of 5% a year, and quarterly sales in 2026 have stayed in a fairly tight band between ¥11,371m and ¥12,120m.
  • For a more cautious take, critics highlight that slower top line growth alongside a softer net margin limits room for error, and the numbers here give them something to point to:
    • The last six reported quarters show revenue fluctuating between ¥10,873m and ¥12,673m, which lines up with the 2.6% annual revenue growth figure and suggests the business is not keeping pace with the broader 5% market expectation.
    • Pairing this with the margin move from 9.3% to 7.9% and Q3 2026 net income of ¥1,230m supports the bearish concern that earnings forecasts around 8.33% a year need to be viewed in light of slower revenue momentum and some pressure on profitability.
Over the last year, Soken’s mix of 2.6% revenue growth and a margin step down to 7.9% has some investors asking whether future earnings can keep pace with the 8.33% growth forecast or whether profit expansion will need more support from pricing or efficiency improvements. 📊 Read the what the Community is saying about Soken Chemical & Engineering.

Trading 36.5% Below DCF Fair Value

  • The shares change hands at ¥2,992 compared with a DCF fair value of ¥4,713.09, a gap of about 36.5%, and the stock trades on a 13.3x P/E versus 14.8x for the Japan chemicals industry and roughly 13.2x for peers.
  • Supporters of a value angle argue that this combination of a wide discount to DCF fair value and slightly cheaper than industry P/E stacks up well against the company’s earnings profile:
    • The trailing 12 month EPS of 225.67 yen per share and five year earnings growth of 13.7% a year provide concrete earnings power to compare with the 13.3x P/E and the ¥2,992 share price.
    • At the same time, the unstable dividend record and the margin move to 7.9% give you clear factors to weigh against the 36.5% DCF fair value gap, especially if you are counting on income or stronger revenue growth than the current 2.6% a year.

data.title: Moderna, Inc. (NASDAQ:MRNA) Released Earnings Last Week And Analysts Lifted Their Price Target To US$40.90
data.description:
As you might know, Moderna, Inc. (NASDAQ:MRNA) just kicked off its latest annual results with some very strong numbers. Moderna beat expectations with revenues of US$1.9b arriving 3.6% ahead of forecasts. The company also reported a statutory loss of US$7.26, 8.0% smaller than was expected. Earnings are an important time for investors, as they can track a company's performance, look at what the analysts are forecasting for next year, and see if there's been a change in sentiment towards the company. So we collected the latest post-earnings statutory consensus estimates to see what could be in store for next year.

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Taking into account the latest results, the most recent consensus for Moderna from 22 analysts is for revenues of US$2.05b in 2026. If met, it would imply a credible 5.3% increase on its revenue over the past 12 months. The loss per share is expected to ameliorate slightly, reducing to US$6.63. Before this latest report, the consensus had been expecting revenues of US$2.04b and US$6.83 per share in losses. So there seems to have been a moderate uplift in analyst sentiment with the latest consensus release, given the upgrade to loss per share forecasts for this year.

View our latest analysis for Moderna

The average price target rose 5.4% to US$40.90, with the analysts signalling that the forecast reduction in losses would be a positive for the stock's valuation. It could also be instructive to look at the range of analyst estimates, to evaluate how different the outlier opinions are from the mean. There are some variant perceptions on Moderna, with the most bullish analyst valuing it at US$135 and the most bearish at US$12.00 per share. We would probably assign less value to the analyst forecasts in this situation, because such a wide range of estimates could imply that the future of this business is difficult to value accurately. data.description:
• NIO, listed as NYSE:NIO, has completed its 100 millionth battery swap, highlighting the scale of its battery swapping network.
  • The company has also announced its largest vehicle recall to date, affecting more than 246,000 cars due to a critical software issue.
  • Both developments come as NIO's share price sits at $4.95, drawing attention to the company’s execution and risk profile.

For investors tracking NYSE:NIO, these updates arrive against a mixed share performance picture. The stock is at $4.95, with a 7-day return of 1.2% and a 30-day return of 5.1%, while the 1-year return stands at 11.5%. Over longer periods, NIO has seen a 3-year return decline of 51.4% and a 5-year return decline of 90.2%, which frames how significant operational milestones and setbacks may be viewed.

As you assess NIO, the combination of a 100 million swap milestone and a large-scale recall raises questions around execution quality, customer confidence, and capital needs. These events can influence how the market weighs NIO's battery swap model, software readiness, and future investments, particularly at current share price levels.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for NIO by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on NIO.

The 100 millionth battery swap and the large recall pull NIO in opposite directions from a risk perspective. On one hand, the swap milestone and record single day volume of 146,649 swaps show that NIO’s battery swap model is being used at scale and has become a core part of the product experience. On the other, a recall of 246,229 vehicles tied to software that can disable key screens highlights how software quality and safety can quickly become a brand and regulatory concern. For you as an investor, the key question is whether the swap network, which is still costly to run, eventually supports margins through services, data and energy management, or whether ongoing capex and quality issues keep weighing on profitability, especially in a market where BYD, Geely and others compete on price and model breadth.

How This Fits Into The NIO Narrative

  • The battery swap milestone supports the narrative that NIO’s proprietary power infrastructure can deepen user engagement and create recurring revenue from energy services and subscriptions.
  • The recall challenges the execution side of that narrative, since higher software and product complexity can increase warranty costs and slow the path to consistent profitability.
  • The scale of grid load shifting and battery diagnostics reported from the swap network is only partly reflected in the narrative and may introduce additional long term optionality around energy services that is not fully captured.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for NIO to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Large scale software recall risk that can increase costs, pressure margins and affect customer perception in a crowded Chinese EV market.
  • ⚠️ Ongoing financial burden of building and operating thousands of swap stations that may keep operating margins behind peers like BYD and Tesla if utilization or monetization fall short of expectations.
  • 🎁 High utilization of the battery swap network and 1 million cumulative deliveries that indicate a sizable installed base to support services and energy related revenue streams over time.
  • 🎁 Management guidance pointing to an adjusted operating profit in Q4 2025 and a goal to break even on an adjusted basis for full year 2026, helped by growing volumes from sub brands Onvo and Firefly.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, you may want to watch three areas. First, how quickly NIO resolves the software issue and whether regulators or customers respond in ways that affect demand. Second, the economics of the battery swap network, including utilization trends at the 3,750 existing stations and the impact of adding 1,000 more in 2026. Third, progress against the company’s own guidance for adjusted operating profit and its break even ambition, especially as competition in China tightens and subsidies roll back. These data points together can help you judge whether the product and infrastructure story behind NIO’s brand is translating into a business that moves closer to sustainable profitability.

Approach 1: CNO Financial Group Excess Returns Analysis

The Excess Returns model looks at how much profit a company generates above the return that equity investors typically require, and then capitalizes those surplus profits into an intrinsic value per share.

For CNO Financial Group, the starting point is its Book Value of US$27.92 per share and a Stable EPS estimate of US$4.15 per share, based on the median return on equity from the past 5 years. The model assumes a Cost of Equity of US$2.57 per share, which implies an Excess Return of US$1.58 per share that is treated as value created over and above the required return.

The analysis also uses a Stable Book Value of US$33.33 per share, sourced from weighted future book value estimates from 3 analysts, and an Average Return on Equity of 12.45%. Together, these inputs are used to project ongoing excess returns and convert them into an estimated intrinsic value of US$70.12 per share.

Compared with the recent share price of US$43.06, the model implies CNO Financial Group is 38.6% undervalued on this basis.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Approach 2: CNO Financial Group Price vs Earnings

P/E is a common way to think about value for profitable companies because it links what you pay directly to what the business is earning per share. In general, higher expected earnings growth and lower perceived risk can support a higher “normal” or “fair” P/E, while slower growth and higher risk usually point to a lower one.

data.title: Melco Resorts & Entertainment Limited Just Beat EPS By 7.6%: Here's What Analysts Think Will Happen Next
data.description:
It's been a sad week for Melco Resorts & Entertainment Limited (NASDAQ:MLCO), who've watched their investment drop 12% to US$5.53 in the week since the company reported its yearly result. The result was positive overall - although revenues of US$5.2b were in line with what the analysts predicted, Melco Resorts & Entertainment surprised by delivering a statutory profit of US$0.46 per share, modestly greater than expected. This is an important time for investors, as they can track a company's performance in its report, look at what experts are forecasting for next year, and see if there has been any change to expectations for the business. Readers will be glad to know we've aggregated the latest statutory forecasts to see whether the analysts have changed their mind on Melco Resorts & Entertainment after the latest results.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

After the latest results, the 13 analysts covering Melco Resorts & Entertainment are now predicting revenues of US$5.32b in 2026. If met, this would reflect a modest 3.0% improvement in revenue compared to the last 12 months. Per-share earnings are expected to climb 17% to US$0.56. Before this earnings report, the analysts had been forecasting revenues of US$5.28b and earnings per share (EPS) of US$0.69 in 2026. So there's definitely been a decline in sentiment after the latest results, noting the substantial drop in new EPS forecasts.

Check out our latest analysis for Melco Resorts & Entertainment

It might be a surprise to learn that the consensus price target fell 5.4% to US$9.73, with the analysts clearly linking lower forecast earnings to the performance of the stock price. The consensus price target is just an average of individual analyst targets, so - it could be handy to see how wide the range of underlying estimates is. The most optimistic Melco Resorts & Entertainment analyst has a price target of US$12.30 per share, while the most pessimistic values it at US$6.40. This is a fairly broad spread of estimates, suggesting that analysts are forecasting a wide range of possible outcomes for the business.

Another way we can view these estimates is in the context of the bigger picture, such as how the forecasts stack up against past performance, and whether forecasts are more or less bullish relative to other companies in the industry. We would highlight that Melco Resorts & Entertainment's revenue growth is expected to slow, with the forecast 3.0% annualised growth rate until the end of 2026 being well below the historical 28% p.a. growth over the last five years. By way of comparison, the other companies in this industry with analyst coverage are forecast to grow their revenue at 9.4% per year. Factoring in the forecast slowdown in growth, it seems obvious that Melco Resorts & Entertainment is also expected to grow slower than other industry participants.

Advertisement

The Bottom Line

The most important thing to take away is that the analysts downgraded their earnings per share estimates, showing that there has been a clear decline in sentiment following these results. On the plus side, there were no major changes to revenue estimates; although forecasts imply they will perform worse than the wider industry. Furthermore, the analysts also cut their price targets, suggesting that the latest news has led to greater pessimism about the intrinsic value of the business.

Following on from that line of thought, we think that the long-term prospects of the business are much more relevant than next year's earnings. We have forecasts for Melco Resorts & Entertainment going out to 2028, and you can see them free on our platform here.
